title: ruellia caroliniensis subsp. ciliosa
intro: Ruellia caroliniensis subsp. ciliosa, also known as the Carolina wild petunia or the tropical petunia, is a flowering plant species native to the southeastern United States. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ruellia caroliniensis subsp. ciliosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to address various health conditions.
